claims.claim_text: In 2022, 37signals publicly described cloud spending well above half a million dollars per year before concluding the economics no longer fit its workload.
claims.span_hint: line 30 | section: Why This Module Matters
claims.rationale: This is a real company-specific cost anecdote, but the primary source is on an off-allowlist domain.

claims.claim_text: Kubernetes uses Pod resource requests when deciding where to place Pods.
claims.span_hint: line 43 | section: From Cloud Waste to Carbon Impact
claims.rationale: The Pods documentation explicitly says the kube-scheduler uses resource requests to decide node placement.

claims.claim_text: Node autoscaling provisions new nodes for unschedulable Pods and consolidates nodes based on Pod resource requests rather than real usage.
claims.span_hint: lines 67-70, 203, 314-319, 555 | sections: From Cloud Waste to Carbon Impact; Right-Sizing and Bin Packing; Autoscaling, Scale-to-Zero, and Zombie Workloads; Worked Example
claims.rationale: The Node Autoscaling docs cover provisioning for unschedulable Pods, consolidation, and the fact that consolidation decisions use requests rather than actual usage.

claims.claim_text: CNCF TAG Environmental Sustainability is the CNCF community group focused on lower-environmental-impact cloud native practice.
claims.span_hint: line 98 | section: The CNCF Sustainability Context
claims.rationale: The TAG's official About page directly describes its purpose and scope.

claims.claim_text: Kepler is a Prometheus exporter for Kubernetes energy metrics at workload and node levels.
claims.span_hint: lines 126, 147, 745 | sections: The CNCF Sustainability Context; Measuring Before Optimizing; Quiz 7 answer
claims.rationale: The Kepler repository README describes it as a Prometheus exporter that measures energy consumption metrics for Kubernetes workloads.

claims.claim_text: Vertical Pod Autoscaler can recommend or set Pod resource requests based on observed usage.
claims.span_hint: line 237 | section: Right-Sizing and Bin Packing
claims.rationale: The VPA quickstart says VPA is ready to recommend and set resource requests for Pods.

claims.claim_text: Goldilocks creates VPAs for workloads and presents their recommendations for review.
claims.span_hint: lines 126, 237 | sections: The CNCF Sustainability Context; Right-Sizing and Bin Packing
claims.rationale: The Goldilocks README states that it creates VPAs and shows their recommendations in the dashboard.

claims.claim_text: KEDA provides event-driven autoscaling for Kubernetes workloads, including scaling to and from zero.
claims.span_hint: lines 126, 289, 328, 627 | sections: The CNCF Sustainability Context; Autoscaling, Scale-to-Zero, and Zombie Workloads; Decision Framework
claims.rationale: The KEDA README describes KEDA as event-driven autoscaling for Kubernetes and explicitly mentions scaling including to and from zero.

claims.claim_text: Knative Serving supports automatic scaling up and down to zero for serverless containers.
claims.span_hint: lines 126, 321, 346 | sections: The CNCF Sustainability Context; Autoscaling, Scale-to-Zero, and Zombie Workloads
claims.rationale: The Knative Serving README explicitly lists automatic scaling up and down to zero among its primitives.

claims.claim_text: The Carbon Aware SDK helps software choose cleaner times or locations to run based on carbon-related energy data.
claims.span_hint: line 126 | section: The CNCF Sustainability Context
claims.rationale: The Carbon Aware SDK README says it helps software choose when and where to run to use greener energy.

claims.claim_text: The module makes an unnecessary version-specific claim that the sample CronJob is runnable in Kubernetes 1.35+.
claims.anchor_text: This CronJob is runnable in a Kubernetes 1.35+ cluster, but the label is only a marker.
claims.suggested_rewrite: This CronJob is runnable in a Kubernetes cluster, but the label is only a marker.
claims.rationale: The exact version floor is not the teaching point here and adds a version-specific claim that does not need to be preserved.

claims.claim_text: Kepler is a CNCF Sandbox project.
claims.rationale: The CNCF project page directly states that Kepler was accepted at the Sandbox maturity level.

further_reading.title: Kubernetes Docs: Node Autoscaling
further_reading.why_relevant: Best primary source for how requests, unschedulable Pods, provisioning, and consolidation interact in cluster-level efficiency work.

further_reading.title: CNCF TAG Environmental Sustainability: About
further_reading.why_relevant: Explains the CNCF community context behind GreenOps and cloud native sustainability work.

further_reading.title: Kepler GitHub Repository
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary upstream reference for workload-level Kubernetes energy telemetry and Prometheus integration.

further_reading.title: Green Software Foundation: Carbon Aware SDK
further_reading.why_relevant: Concrete reference for temporal and regional carbon-aware execution decisions discussed in the module.

notes: No shared citation pool exists for this KCNA section, so all supported claims point directly to authoritative URLs. No claim entries were created for hypothetical teaching scenarios, code blocks, quizzes, hands-on exercise steps, or most tables unless a row or sentence made a named project or version-specific factual claim.
